root.name: Successfully create a table with two columns and convert the checklist into table format on slide 8
root.description: Evaluates whether the agent has correctly created a two-column table on slide 8 and converted the checklist items into table format, without making extraneous changes elsewhere.

children.name: Correct table structure exists on slide 8
children.description: Checks if a table with exactly two columns is present on slide 8.

children.name: Checklist items are converted to table rows
children.description: Checks that the checklist items from the original slide 8 are now present as rows in the table on slide 8 (not simply copied as a text box).

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    from pptx import Presentation
    import re
    prs_orig = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    prs_mod = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    slide_orig = prs_orig.slides[7]
    slide_mod = prs_mod.slides[7]

    # Find checklist items in original slide (look for bullet points or checklist like lines)
    checklist_items = ['Figurative language – metaphors, personification, similes, imagery', 
                       'Rhyme, end rhyme, iambic pentameter, rhythm, rhyming couplet',
                       'Punctuation, commas, pause, caesura',
                       'Form and stanza structure, sonnet (8/6), ode, ballad, blank verse',
                       'Repetition',
                       'Tone']

    # Find table in modified slide
    table_items = []
    for shape in slide_mod.shapes:
        if shape.shape_type == 19:  # MSO_SHAPE_TYPE.TABLE
            table = shape.table
            for row in table.rows:
                row_text = [cell.text.strip() for cell in row.cells if cell.text.strip()]
                table_items.append(row_text)

    # Flatten table_items for easier matching
    flat_table_texts = set([cell for row in table_items for cell in row])
    matched = [item for item in checklist_items if any(cell_item in flat_table_texts for cell_item in [item, item.lstrip('-•□✓')])]
    if len(checklist_items) == 0:
        return "No checklist items found on original slide 8.", 0.0
    ratio = len(matched) / len(checklist_items)
    if ratio == 1.0:
        return "All checklist items converted to table rows.", 1.0
    elif ratio > 0.7:
        return f"{len(matched)} of {len(checklist_items)} checklist items converted to table rows.", ratio
    else:
        return f"Only {len(matched)} of {len(checklist_items)} checklist items converted to table rows.", ratio

children.name: No extraneous changes on other slides
children.description: Checks that no slides other than slide 8 were modified, added, or removed.

children.name: No extraneous content changes on slide 8
children.description: Checks that except for the table conversion, no significant extraneous content was added or deleted on slide 8.

scorer.function_code:
def compute_score() -> tuple[str, float]:
    # If only table added and checklist text removed, that's expected. But other shapes added/removed = penalize.
    from pptx import Presentation
    prs_orig = Presentation(original_ppt_path)
    prs_mod = Presentation(modified_ppt_path)
    slide_orig = prs_orig.slides[7]
    slide_mod = prs_mod.slides[7]
    orig_shape_types = [sh.shape_type for sh in slide_orig.shapes]
    mod_shape_types = [sh.shape_type for sh in slide_mod.shapes]
    orig_num_shapes = len(slide_orig.shapes)
    mod_num_shapes = len(slide_mod.shapes)
    # Expect 1 extra shape (the table), and possibly the checklist text box removed
    expected_delta = 0
    table_added = any(sh.shape_type == 19 for sh in slide_mod.shapes) and not any(sh.shape_type == 19 for sh in slide_orig.shapes)
    checklist_removed = orig_num_shapes > mod_num_shapes

    # Count extra added/removed shapes
    added = mod_num_shapes - orig_num_shapes
    # If more than 1 shape difference, penalize
    if abs(added) > 1:
        return f"Extraneous content changes detected on slide 8 (shape count delta={added}).", max(0.0, 1.0 - abs(added)/5)
    elif not table_added:
        return "No table added to slide 8.", 0.0
    else:
        return "No significant extraneous content changes on slide 8.", 1.0

metadata.task: Create a table with two columns and convert the checklist into table format on slide 8
